step.title: Update Bundle Files
step.goal: Persist this turn's findings, Q&A entries, glossary resolutions, and Decision Records to disk. Stamp last_updated on every modified file. The bundle on disk IS the handoff state — if the session ends here, another session can resume cleanly.

substep.title: Append Synthesis to Research File
Append Q&A Entry to Questions File

check.action:
Invoke the context-md-format skill via the skill system. Pass: - List of terms to add or update - The repo's current glossary state ({{session.glossary}}) - The repo root path
context-md-format will: - Lazy-create CONTEXT.md if it does not exist - Append or update entries inline - Maintain the opinionated-naming and Avoid-list discipline

check.ask:
This turn's mode includes Prototyping. The gap to explore is: {{session.turn_focus}}
Run a throwaway prototype now? - Yes (Recommended): invoke the ticket-prototype skill; findings will be appended to the research file alongside this turn's other findings - No: skip prototyping; absorb whatever findings the research-agenda dispatches produced - Defer: log the prototype need in the questions file for a future turn

check.action:
On return, take the findings_block from ticket-prototype's return and APPEND it to {{session.research_path}} (Edit tool — append after this turn's synthesis from substep 5a). The block follows ticket-prototype's defined shape (### Prototype: ... section).
If ticket-prototype returned status: "aborted" or "escalate_to_spike", do NOT append a findings_block (it's null); instead, log the abort or spike-recommendation in the questions file's R-entry for this turn (already written in 5b — add an append).
Store the prototype invocation in {{session.turn_prototype_record}}: {name, path, status} for use in subsequent substeps.
Note in {{session.turn_prototype_record}} that prototyping was offered and declined/deferred. Continue without invoking the skill.
Skip this substep. No prototype this turn.

check.ask:
This turn surfaced a hard-to-reverse decision matching the three-condition gate:
- Title: {{session.dr_candidate.title}} - Hard to reverse: yes - Surprising without context: yes - Real trade-off: yes ({{considered_options summary}})
Record as a Decision Record (DR)? - Yes (Recommended): scaffold the DR file now; link from the ticket - No: capture in Q&A log only; do not create a separate artifact

check.action:
Invoke the decision-record-format skill to draft the DR body (title, context, decision, why, optional considered options).
Then invoke scaffold-decision-record.py to create the DR file with schema-valid frontmatter:
uv run {base_dir}/scripts/scaffold-decision-record.py --slug "{{dr_slug}}" --title "{{dr_title}}" --parent-ticket-alias "ticket-{{date}}-{{session.slug}}"
Capture the returned DR path and DR number from the manifest.
Edit the ticket file frontmatter to add the DR's canonical alias to the `children:` array.
Write the DR body content (drafted via decision-record-format) into the scaffolded DR file using the Edit tool.
Note the rejected DR in the Q&A entry (already in 5b). Do not scaffold a file. Continue.

substep.title: Stamp last_updated on Modified Files
substep.action:
For each modified file this turn (research file always; questions file always; ticket file if children was edited; CONTEXT.md if terms were added; any new DR), invoke:
uv run {base_dir}/scripts/stamp-frontmatter.py --file "{{file_path}}" --note "Turn {{turn_number}}: {{turn_focus_short}}"
Per Lock 27, stamp-frontmatter.py REPLACES last_updated_note (git holds history).

check.action: Halt the loop. Surface validation errors. Do NOT auto-fix; the script or the skill that wrote invalid frontmatter is the bug.

substep.title: Display Bundle Update Summary

section.title: Write continuously, not at session end
section.description: Per spec §6, CONTEXT.md updates are INLINE during Q↔R — not batched at session end. Same principle applies to Q&A entries, research synthesis, and DRs. Every turn writes its outputs to disk before moving to step 6. The bundle on disk IS the handoff state; a partially-written turn is recoverable, an in-memory turn is lost on context death.

rules.rule: The research and questions files exist from ticket-orient — APPEND with Edit, never overwrite with Write
The ticket file's frontmatter is edited (children array, last_updated) with Edit, never overwritten
CONTEXT.md is appended to or updated entry-by-entry with Edit (lazy-create is the only Write operation, handled inside context-md-format skill)
New DR files use scaffold-decision-record.py to create the shell, then Edit to fill the body

section.title: DR scaffolding is gated by both rules AND user approval
section.description: The three-condition gate is a NECESSARY condition for DR creation, not a sufficient one. Even if all three are true, the user must explicitly approve. This catches cases where the gate technically fires but the user knows context the agent does not (e.g., the decision is "hard to reverse" only in theory because the company is about to migrate anyway).

section.title: When step 5 has nothing to do
section.description: A turn might be light: a quick research dispatch returns "no, X does not exist", no user question, no glossary update, no DR. Even then, step 5 runs — the research file gets an appended Turn entry with the negative finding, the questions file gets an R-entry, and last_updated is stamped. Negative findings are evidence; they belong on disk.
